decision: Implement live WS status stream for the Attention board, overriding the prior polling-only deferral.
context: Watchtower /ws is a broadcast bus (rebroadcasts every client message to all other clients), reachable only server-side via WATCHTOWER_API_URL in container mode.
choice: Host the browser-facing bridge in the existing terminal server (port 4201), dispatched by ?stream=attention. Reuse the terminal WS upgrade auth (origin + Traefik-forwarded / single-use bearer ticket / loopback bypass) verbatim via authenticateConnection. Server dials Watchtower /ws and relays protocol frames read-only (inbound client frames ignored, never forwarded onto the bus).
alternatives: Next.js route-handler WS (not viable in App Router); direct browser->Watchtower (impossible in container mode).
client: Extended useAttentionPoll: REST snapshot seeds the full card set (source of truth); live deltas applied via pure reducer lib/attention/live.ts; poll is the resilient fallback when WS is down; snapshot re-fetched on every (re)connect to resync; exponential reconnect backoff; paused when tab hidden; single-flight snapshot; unknown message types ignored (forward-compatible).
auth: No new app/api route (bridge lives in terminal server), so audit:auth is unaffected; container mode uses the existing /api/terminal/ticket mint; host-dev uses loopback bypass with no secret.
assumption: Watchtower /ws shares the REST port (main.go serves both on the same mux), so the WS URL is watchtowerBaseUrl() with http->ws and /ws appended.
